title: Methods for radiation therapy quality assurance and verification
abstract: This disclosure relates to phantoms for the calibration and quality assurance of radiation therapy systems, including biologically-guided radiation therapy systems, which deliver doses of radiation in response to real-time detected PET lines-of-response (LORs). The phantoms disclosed herein are modular phantoms that comprise a cylindrical housing with a plurality of disks stacked within the housing and a plurality of radiographic films. Some disks have a background region and a target region, where the rate of positron emissions of the target region is higher than for the background region. The disks may be arranged and rearranged within the housing to attain a desired target region shape.
claims: Claims 1. A phantom disk comprising: an enclosed shell having an internal volume; a first region of the volume comprising a first material; and a second region of the volume comprising a second material that is more radioactive than the first material. 2. The disk of claim 1, wherein the second material emits more positrons than the first material. 3. The disk of claim 2, wherein the first material has a first standard uptake value and the second material has a second standard uptake value that is greater than the first standard uptake value. 4. The disk of claim 3, wherein the second standard uptake value is about four times greater than the first standard uptake value. 5. The disk of claim 1, wherein the first material comprises Ge-68 epoxy that has a first standard uptake value and the second material comprises Ge-68 epoxy that has a second standard uptake value that is about four times greater than the first standard uptake value. 6. The disk of claim 5, wherein the second region has a smaller volume than the first region. 7. The disk of claim 6, wherein the second region has a circular cross-section. 8. The disk of claim 6, wherein the second region has an arc-shaped cross-section. 9. The disk of claim 1, wherein the disk has a thickness and the second region extends across the entire thickness of the disk. 10. The disk of claim 1, wherein the internal volume of the shell is filled with a radioactive liquid. 11. The disk of claim 1, wherein the internal volume of the shell is filled with a radioactive solid or radioactive gel. 12. A phantom module comprising: a housing comprising a longitudinal lumen therethrough; a plurality of phantom disks stacked within the longitudinal lumen; a plurality of dosimetry sensors disposed between the plurality of phantom disks; and a cap that encloses the longitudinal lumen such that the phantom disks and the dosimetry sensors are retained and compressed within the lumen. 13. The phantom module of claim 12, wherein at least one of the plurality of phantom disks comprise a background region and a target region, wherein the target region is more radioactive than the background region. 14. The phantom module of claim 13, where the target region of the at least one of the plurality of phantom disks has a higher rate of positron emissions than the background region. 15. The phantom module of claim 14, wherein the standard uptake value of the target region is greater than the standard uptake value of the background region. 16. The phantom module of claim 15, wherein the standard uptake value of the target region is four times greater than the standard uptake value of the background region. 17. The phantom module of claim 13, wherein the target region has a circular cross-section. 18. The phantom module of claim 13, wherein the target region has an arc-shaped cross- section. 19. The phantom module of claim 12, wherein at least one of the plurality of phantom disks comprises a radiopaque material. 20. The phantom module of claim 12, wherein the dosimetry sensors comprise radiographic film.
